cards.title: Swahili Greeting
cards.body: In Swahili, habari (hah-BAH-ree) means 'hello' or 'greetings' — literally 'news', as in 'what's the news?'. Swahili is written in the Latin alphabet, and a warm reply is nzuri (n-ZOO-ree), meaning 'good'.

cards.title: Polite in Thai
cards.body: In Thai, ค่ะ (kha) (KAH) is used to make a statement polite when speaking to someone older or in a position of authority.

cards.title: Counting in Japanese
cards.body: In Japanese, the number one is 一 (ichi) (EE-chee), two is 二 (ni) (NEE), and three is 三 (san) (SAHN).

cards.title: Goodbye in Arabic
cards.body: In Arabic, مع السلامة (ma'a as-salaama) (MA-ah ah-sah-LAH-mah) means 'goodbye' or 'go with peace'.

cards.title: Please in Spanish
cards.body: In Spanish, por favor (pohr fah-VOHR) means 'please', and is used to be polite when asking for something.

cards.title: Etymology of 'Dictionary'
cards.body: The word 'dictionary' comes from the Latin 'dictionarium', meaning 'a collection of words'.

cards.title: Linguistic Diversity
cards.body: There are over 7,000 languages spoken worldwide, with many more dialects and regional variations.

cards.title: Chinese Tones
cards.body: Mandarin Chinese has four main tones: flat (first tone), rising (second tone), falling then rising (third tone), and sharply falling (fourth tone).

cards.title: Korean Greeting
cards.body: In Korean, 안녕하세요 (annyeonghaseyo) (AHN-yohng-hah-seh-yoh) is a formal greeting, while 안녕 (annyeong) (AHN-yohng) is more informal.

cards.title: Russian Alphabet
cards.body: The Russian alphabet, also known as Cyrillic, consists of 33 letters and is used to write many languages, including Russian, Ukrainian, and Belarusian.

cards.title: German Grammar
cards.body: German grammar has four cases: nominative, accusative, genitive, and dative, which can be challenging for non-native speakers to learn.

cards.title: Portuguese Pronunciation
cards.body: In Portuguese, the letter 'c' is pronounced as /k/ when followed by 'a', 'o', or 'u', but as /s/ when followed by 'e' or 'i'.

quizzes.question: What does 'habari' mean in Swahili?
quizzes.explanation: Habari is a Swahili greeting that means 'hello' or 'greetings'.

quizzes.question: What is the Japanese word for the number one?
quizzes.explanation: The Japanese word for the number one is 一 (ichi).

quizzes.question: What is the origin of the word 'dictionary'?
quizzes.explanation: The word 'dictionary' comes from the Latin 'dictionarium', meaning 'a collection of words'.

quizzes.question: How many letters are in the Russian alphabet?
quizzes.explanation: The Russian alphabet, also known as Cyrillic, consists of 33 letters.
